title: RCMP National Chemical Precursor Diversion Program - Canada.ca
content:
## RCMP National Chemical Precursor Diversion Program
To address the problem of chemical diversion, the RCMP instituted the 'National Chemical Precursor Diversion Program'. The role of the program coordinators is to liaise with Health Canada and the chemical industry, assist drug investigators conducting clandestine laboratory investigations, and provide training to the chemical industry thus allowing companies and research organizations to identify, monitor and prevent suspicious transactions.
Chemical companies interested in more information on the RCMP National Chemical Precursor Diversion Program should contact:
Royal Canadian Mounted Police "K" Division 11140 109 th Street Edmonton, Alberta T5G 2T4 E-mail: RCMP_Alberta_ChemDivert@rcmp-grc.gc.ca
Royal Canadian Mounted Police Drug Enforcement Branch, "E" Division 14200 Green Timbers Way Surrey, British Columbia V3T 6P3 Email: bc_chem_diversion@rcmp-grc.gc.ca
Royal Canadian Mounted Police Combined Forces Special Enforcement Unit (CFSEU) – "C" Division 4225 Dorchester blvd, Westmount, Québec H3Z 1V5 E-mail: detournement-chimique-qc-chemical-diversion@rcmp-grc.gc.ca
Royal Canadian Mounted Police Criminal Operations, "O" Division 2755 High Point Road Milton, Ontario L9T 5E8 E-mail: odiv_ChemWatch@rcmp-grc.gc.ca

## National Headquarter
Royal Canadian Mounted Police National HQ 73 Leikin Drive Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0R2 Email: fpco_soc-ocpf_cgco@rcmp-grc.gc.ca

## Chemical companies can also reach out to provincial  programs:
Ontario  Provincial Police Email: OPP.Chemical.Diversion@opp.ca
Sûreté du Québec Email : sq-info-precurseurs@surete.qc.ca
